report_source_files: Old Dominion University Common Data Set 2024-2025
Old Dominion University Common Data Set 2025-2026

strategic_profile.executive_summary: Old Dominion University (ODU) is a progressive, metropolitan public research university located in Norfolk, Virginia. Holding an R1 Carnegie Classification for very high research activity, ODU provides a highly accessible educational environment with a strong emphasis on engineering, business, health sciences, and cybersecurity.

analyst_takeaways.insight: With an acceptance rate consistently above 90%, ODU is an accessible institution that offers open opportunities for students seeking entry into a research-focused university.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students with average academic profiles are highly likely to be admitted, making it a reliable target or safety school.

analyst_takeaways.insight: ODU maintains a heavy investment in modern, high-demand disciplines, establishing dedicated schools such as the School of Cybersecurity and the School of Data Science.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants targeting tech, maritime, and defense-adjacent careers will find strong institutional alignment and employer pipelines.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university has a substantial commuter and part-time population, alongside a very diverse student body and strong military affiliation.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The social environment is multifaceted, requiring proactive engagement to fully experience traditional campus life.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and highly diverse, though heavily influenced by a large commuter and non-traditional student population. Strong school spirit is centered around Division I athletics.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in the Hampton Roads region, proximity to naval bases and maritime centers heavily impacts local internship and career opportunities.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 Doctoral University, ODU drives significant research initiatives, notably in bioelectronics, modeling and simulation, and coastal resilience.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test optional. SAT or ACT scores are not required for admission but are considered if submitted. Scores may be used for academic advising and math/writing placement.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed for Fall 2025.

gpa.notes: Average high school GPA reported for degree-seeking enrollees.

testing.policy_note: Only 7% of enrolled students submitted SAT scores and 1% submitted ACT scores for Fall 2025.

colleges.name: Ellmer College of Health Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students pursuing nursing must be highly competitive, maintaining prerequisite GPAs well above minimums.

majors.name: Nursing (Prelicensure BSN)
majors.special_requirements: All non-nursing education prerequisite courses must be fully completed before admission to the nursing major.
majors.notes: Admission is highly competitive; while the minimum GPA is 3.0, admitted cohorts routinely have at least a 3.6 GPA in prerequisites.

colleges.name: College of Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Tech-focused students looking for affordable, high-research-activity programs.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: 300 and 400 level CS courses require C++ proficiency. Transfer courses not taught in C++ do not meet upper-level prerequisites.

colleges.name: Strome College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students looking to tap into Hampton Roads' maritime, logistics, and management networks.

colleges.name: Batten College of Engineering and Technology
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students interested in aerospace, civil, and environmental engineering.

majors.name: Engineering
majors.special_requirements: Chemistry Placement Exam required unless specific transfer/AP credits (MATH 102M/103M and CHEM 105/106N) are met.

application_deadlines.notes: Nonbinding Early Action plan; notifications by Jan 15.
Fall admission deadline. Applications received after are evaluated on a rolling basis.

supplemental_requirements.details: Students must apply to the highly-competitive BSN program separately from their general ODU acceptance.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: As a highly accessible public university, quantitative metrics (GPA/rigor) are the dominant drivers of admission.

major_selection_tactics: Direct admission into ODU does not guarantee admission to specialized clinical programs like Dental Hygiene or Nursing.
Pre-nursing students should focus on attaining at least a 3.6 GPA in early prerequisite courses to ensure smooth internal transfer.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying undeclared is viable given the open admissions landscape, though internal transfer to impacted health sciences requires high baseline performance in introductory sciences.

financials.tuition_model: Per-credit hour scaling up to a flat rate for full-time enrollment

scholarships.name: Haislip-Rorrer Presidential Scholars Endowment
scholarships.benefits: Financial assistance for entering freshmen with outstanding academic credentials (generally 3.4+ GPA).

scholarships.name: Transfer Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable award for in-state transfer students who earned an associate degree with a 4.0 cumulative GPA.

scholarships.name: Transfer Monarch Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable award for in-state transfer students who earned an associate degree with a 3.8-3.9 cumulative GPA.

ap_policy.general_rule: ODU awards advanced standing credit for qualifying AP exam scores.
ap_policy.usage: Credits may bypass placement exams (e.g., Chemistry Placement or Writing Success Placement Tool) and satisfy prerequisites.

ib_policy.general_rule: Recognized for credit similarly to AP courses.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Remedial coursework does not transfer. Must earn a 'C' or better for equivalent credit. ODU has Guaranteed Admission Agreements with the Virginia Community College System.

what_it_takes: A baseline high school GPA around 3.0+ secures admission, while 3.5+ unlocks internal admission to competitive pre-clinical tracks.
Completing all required non-nursing education courses before applying for major declaration.
common_activities: Greek Life (5% participation for both men and women)
essay_tips: Optional for admission, but can provide contextual support for academic advising or scholarship consideration.
red_flags: Submitting an application with a GPA significantly below the 3.0 threshold without contextual explanations.
insights: The campus maintains a significant commuter demographic; only 24% of all undergraduates live on campus.
A quarter of the ODU student body is affiliated with the U.S. military, fostering a mature and highly structured campus environment.
Adjunct professors are frequently utilized in lower-level classes, leading some students to feel disconnected from full-time faculty early on.
